---
title: "PhysMove: Intraspecific Movements"
author: "Hannah Calich, Jorge Rodríguez, Víctor Eguíluz & Ana Sequeira"
date: "Last updated: `r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  officedown::rdocx_document:
    reference_docx: "template.docx"
vignette: |
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{PhysMove: Intraspecific Movements}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
---

<style>
      img {
      border: 0;
    }
</style>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(#dpi = 600, 
                      fig.align = 'center', 
                      #fig.width = 4, 
                      #fig.height = 3,
                      echo = TRUE,
                      collapse = TRUE,
                      fig.path = "images/",
                      comment = "#>") 
```

<img src="../vignettes/PhysMoveHex.png" align="right" width="130" />

```{r load physmove, echo=FALSE}
# Load PhysMove
library(PhysMove)
```

```{r gyrad, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE}
# Calculate the dispersion of each track in the tracks dataset
GR <- gyrationRad(tracks)
```

```{r preview gyrad, echo=TRUE}
# Summarize gyration radius results
summary(GR)
```

```{r summary gr, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} 
library(kableExtra)

table <- as.data.frame.matrix(summary(GR))
row.names(table) <- NULL

table %>%
    kable("html", align="l") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed"), full_width = F, html_font = "Consolas", font_size = 35) %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(1:3, width_min = "400px") %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(4, width_min = "985px") %>%
    kableExtra::row_spec(0:nrow(table), background = "#F8F8F8", color = "#60a0b0", extra_css = "padding-top: 0px !important; padding-bottom: 0px !important") %>%
    kableExtra::save_kable("vignettes/word_formatted/tables/summary_gyrad.png", zoom=1) # html files cant be output to word
```

```{r gyrad_pdf, echo=TRUE}
# Create a pdf plot of gyration radius values
pdf.gr <- plotPDF((GR$`rG (km)`), desc="gyrationRad")
```
  
```{r ent, echo=TRUE}
# Calculate track entropy using default parameters
Ent <- entropy(tracks)
```
 
```{r ent head, echo=TRUE}
# Summarise Entropy results
summary(Ent)
```

```{r summary ent, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} 
library(kableExtra)

table <- as.data.frame.matrix(summary(Ent))
row.names(table) <- NULL

table %>%
    kable("html", align="l") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed"), full_width = F, html_font = "Consolas", font_size = 35) %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(1:3, width_min = "400px") %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(4, width_min = "985px") %>%
    kableExtra::row_spec(0:nrow(table), background = "#F8F8F8", color = "#60a0b0", extra_css = "padding-top: 0px !important; padding-bottom: 0px !important") %>%
    kableExtra::save_kable("vignettes/word_formatted/tables/summary_ent.png", zoom=1) # html files cant be output to word
```

```{r ent_pdf, echo=TRUE}
# Create a pdf plot of the entropy scores
pdf.ent <- plotPDF(Ent$normalisedEntropy, "entropy")
```

```{r predict, echo=TRUE}
# Track predictability using Predictability() default parameters and the output from Entropy()
Pred <- predictability(tracks, Ent)
```
 
```{r predict head, echo=TRUE}
# Summarize Predictability scores
summary(Pred)
```

```{r summary predict, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} 
library(kableExtra)

table <- as.data.frame.matrix(summary(Pred))
row.names(table) <- NULL

table %>%
    kable("html", align="l") %>%
    kableExtra::kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("condensed"), full_width = F, html_font = "Consolas", font_size = 35) %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(1, width_min = "400px") %>%
    kableExtra::column_spec(2, width_min = "1785px") %>% 
    kableExtra::row_spec(0:nrow(table), background = "#F8F8F8", color = "#60a0b0", extra_css = "padding-top: 0px !important; padding-bottom: 0px !important") %>%
    kableExtra::save_kable("vignettes/word_formatted/tables/summary_predict.png", zoom=1) # html files cant be output to word
```

```{r predict_pdf, echo=TRUE}
# Create a pdf plot of the predictability scores
pdf.pred <- plotPDF(Pred$Predictability, desc="predictability")
```
